page: Microsoft Azure DC8s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DCSv3 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The server features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache, alongside support for AVX-512 and cryptography instructions such as AES and SHA_NI.

A key architectural tradeoff of the DC8s_v3 is the complete absence of local storage and dedicated GPU hardware. Storage requirements must be met using external Premium Storage, which the instance is capable of supporting. The lack of a physical GPU limits its utility for heavy graphics processing or large-scale parallel computing, though it supports vector extensions like AVX-512 for CPU-based mathematical operations.

Benchmark data places the DC8s_v3 in the average performance tier across most standardized workloads. It achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1688.0 and a multi-core score of 9024.0, while the Passmark CPU score reached 17073.48. In memory bandwidth testing, the instance recorded 87.04 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 48.79 GB/sec for large-block write operations. For database workloads, it processed 16.96 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 7752.84 in Passmark database operations. Notably, the server demonstrated strong performance in text generation for small language models, achieving 279.29 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC8s_v3 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, making it a balanced option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute or local NVMe storage. The instance is suited for database operations, memory-cached applications, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure DC8s_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor clocked at 2.8 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9024.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 17073.48. It demonstrates strong performance in small-scale LLM text generation, reaching 279.29 tokens per second. With an 8.0 GB per core memory allocation, this server is suited for database operations, memory-cached applications, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC8s_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, suited for database and memory-cached workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC8s_v3 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 Intel Xeon cores, 64.0 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for memory-intensive tasks.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, dedicated Intel Xeon cores, and support for premium external storage.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core)
No local storage or dedicated GPU included
Strong text generation performance for small language models
Suited for database operations, caching, and software compilation
